title: Semantic Similarity in Scholarly Text Evaluation
abstract: This study presents a transformer-based approach for evaluating semantic similarity in academic manuscripts. The method uses sentence embeddings, cosine similarity, and ranked retrieval over a scholarly corpus to detect concept-level overlap beyond lexical matching.

title: Paraphrase-Aware Academic Integrity Detection
abstract: We propose a paraphrase-sensitive framework for scholarly integrity analysis. The system matches sentence pairs using contextual embeddings and identifies structural rewriting that preserves core meaning.

title: Explainable Detection of AI-Generated Academic Writing
abstract: This work studies AI-generated text detection in research-style prose using stylometric indicators, entropy analysis, and interpretable feature attributions. The framework reports reasons behind each classification decision.

title: Hybrid Forensic Analysis for Research Originality
abstract: A hybrid forensic pipeline combines semantic retrieval, paraphrase evidence, and AI-likelihood estimation into a unified originality score. The system emphasizes explainability and reviewer-facing evidence mapping.

title: Contextual Embeddings for Scientific Document Comparison
abstract: Contextual sentence representations can improve scientific document comparison by capturing long-range meaning and methodological similarity. This approach supports top-k retrieval and section-aware evidence extraction.
